title: Detection of viable Dichelobacter nodosus by real-time PCR using PMAxx™
author: Peter Kuhnert
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbh9ij96
materials: VetMAX™ Xeno™ Internal Positive Control DNA

steps.instruction: place or dissolve your sample containing Dichelobacter nodosus in a total voulme of 50 µl 0.85% NaCl in a transparent 1.5 ml tube
in a darkened room add 100 µM (0.25 µl) of PMAxx™ to the tube
vortex and incubate for 3 min at room temperature in a metal box impervious to light
after incubation, place tube on ice and expose to LED light (white light, 100 W, 6000 K, 8500 lm) at a distance of 20 cm for 5 min
centrifuge tube at 15'000xg for 5 min in a microcentrifuge
to remove the supernatant, the tubes can be held in a horizontal position and twisted tissues used to absorb the liquid
resuspend pellet in 500 µl SV-lysis buffer (4 M guanidine thiocyanate, 0.01M Tris–HCl, 1% β-mercaptoethanol)
add 2 µl of VetMAX™ Xeno™ Internal Positive Control DNA to the sample in SV-lysis buffer (this is the extraction control and later also the internal PCR control)
add 30 µl MagneSil®RED and resuspend without vortexing
extract DNA using KingFisher™ Duo Prime Purification System (or an alternative magnetic bead based system) and elute the purified DNA in 60 µl H 2 O
primers and probes as well as the real-time PCR are described in the publications 116. Dnodosus PCR.pdf 140. Dnodosus infection SAT.pdf

prepare 25 µl containing: - 1x TaqMan™ Fast Advanced Master Mix - 300 nM primer DnAprTM-L (CAATAGCCAAATTTCTTTAGATGGTGAT) - 300 nM DnAprTM-R (CAAGAGCTGTCGCTTCTTTCTTT) - 100 nM Probe DnAprTm-v (FAM-CGGTGGTTATCCTGAT-MGB) - 250 nM Probe DnAprTM-b (VIC-TGGTCGTCCTGATC-MGB) - pyrogen-free water - 1 µl Xeno™ LIZ Primer Probe Mix and 2.5 µl of DNA template
prepare at least duplicates for each sample and run on an appropriate instrument like 7500 Real-Time PCR-System instrument
run using cycles of 2 min at 50°C and 10 min at 95°C followed by 40 cycles with 15 s at 95°C and 1 min at 60°C
results are analyzed using the Sequence Detector 7500 software (v 2.3.) with the threshold set at 0.015

signals will only result from alive D. nodosus and no dead cell will be detected. For absolute quantification a standard curve using defined amounts of D. nodosus genome equivalents can be generaded in parallel
